architecture_fit.note: Not a model at all: closes over the seed-0 true_states and ignores tokens — no transformer, no delta from base_model.py, no state tracking.

baseline_comparison.note: No strawman contrast shown; only the auto-included random baseline in the payload, with no method-vs-baseline demonstration.

faithfulness.note: Anti-faithful: it memorizes the labels rather than computing them; no ablation/patching and no concrete causal check proposed.

operating_range.note: Hardcoded to the canonical [128,64] seed-0 batch; would break or be wrong on any other seed/scale — zero operating range.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: N/A: it hardcodes the answers, not a mechanism/circuit, so it provides no evidence of understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab renders tokens/true/pred/accuracy, but pred==true is trivially guaranteed by the oracle, so the chart makes an empty claim.

visualisation_rationale.note: README has a 'Why this visualisation' section, but it rationalizes a trivial oracle rather than a real tracking mechanism.

notes: This 'first_pass' is a degenerate oracle that closes over the canonical seed-0 true states and ignores its token input, so its perfect benchmark (robustness 1.0) reflects memorization of the answer key, not FSM state tracking. It is not a transformer, not a delta from base_model.py, has no operating range beyond the canonical batch, and offers no baseline or causal evidence. Honest in the README about being an oracle, but as an interpretability result it answers none of the goal's question.
